name: TAO Compression Fakeout Reversion v1
thesis: TAO 4h is the best structurally different lane for testing a compression fakeout that resolves back into balance rather than into continuation. The edge should come from role reassignment: compression is only a setup, while reacceptance does the directional work.
invalidation_condition: Abort if the compression setup is too rare, if the fakeout condition and value reacceptance conflict, or if reversions do not reach balance before time decay.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A compressed market fakes one side of balance, fails to continue, and rotates back once value is reaccepted.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In chop, compression often precedes failed directional probes rather than genuine expansion. TAO gives a structurally different lane to test whether that behavior is stronger away from DOGE/ETH.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is appropriate because the lane is distinct enough to test the role reassignment cleanly while remaining inside the authorized basket.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If TAO resolves compressions via genuine expansion instead of failed probes, the reversion thesis will be on the wrong side of the move.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If setups appear but reversions are shallow, lower the take-profit expectation before narrowing the compression threshold.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: roles are intentionally separated; compression is setup only, EMA close is direction
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: kept manageable by moderate compression threshold rather than extreme squeeze-only logic

long: Take profit: EMA_20 + 0.9 * ATR_14 equivalent move toward balance
Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.25 * ATR_14
short: Take profit: EMA_20 - 0.9 * ATR_14 equivalent move toward balance
Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.25 * ATR_14
